channel.title: single cell genomics on Not A Rocket Scientist
channel.description: Recent content in single cell genomics on Not A Rocket Scientist

item.title: Project scg_lib_structs
item.description: Being able to perform genomic studies on the material obtained from single cells is just awesome. In 2009, Tang et al. presented the first study of mRNA-seq from single cells (scRNA-seq). Two years later, Navin et al. successfully sequenced the genome of single cells. Ever since then, the field of single cell genomics is rapidly evolving and has proved to be powerful in many aspects of biology. Many methods (mainly for scRNA-seq) are invented along the way, which is a good thing.
